name: Pomegranate Raita
tagline: Whisked yogurt with pomegranate, mint, and roasted cumin — antioxidant cool-down
highlight: Pomegranate's punicalagins survive in yogurt — antioxidant power without cooking

hero.alt: White raita in a bowl studded with red pomegranate seeds and chopped mint
hero.caption: pomegranate raita — ruby seeds, cooling yogurt

healthFacts: Pomegranate's punicalagins are among the most potent food-source antioxidants.
Vitamin C, K, folate, and potassium all hit meaningful levels per serving.
Yogurt's probiotic strains support gut health and B-vitamin synthesis.
Raw + no heat = full nutrient retention from the pomegranate.

steps.title: Whisk the yogurt
steps.detail: Whisk yogurt with a splash of water in a bowl until smooth and slightly pourable.
steps.tip: Cold yogurt straight from the fridge — warmer yogurt thins the raita.

steps.title: Season
steps.detail: Stir in roasted cumin powder, black salt, and chaat masala if using. Taste — should be balanced between salty and tangy.
steps.tip: Roast cumin yourself for the best aroma — store-bought roasted cumin loses its top notes.

steps.title: Fold and serve
steps.detail: Add pomegranate seeds, mint, and coriander. Fold gently — don't mash the seeds. Serve immediately or chill 10 minutes.
steps.tip: The seeds stay crunchy for 30 minutes. Make this last.
